name: E-anna
description: E-anna, meaning "House of Heaven" or "House of Anu," was a prominent temple complex located in Uruk, one of the most important cities in ancient Sumer. It was dedicated primarily to Inanna (Ishtar in Akkadian), the goddess of love, beauty, sex, war, justice, and political power. The E-anna was not merely a religious center but also a vital political and economic hub, reflecting the close intertwining of religious and secular life in Mesopotamian society. The temple complex underwent numerous phases of construction and reconstruction throughout its history, with each successive ruler seeking to expand and embellish it, demonstrating their piety and power. It played a crucial role in Uruk's rise to prominence. The E-anna's legendary status is reinforced by its appearance in numerous Sumerian myths, including tales of Inanna's adventures and her role in bestowing civilization upon humanity. It was believed to be the earthly residence of Inanna, a place where mortals could interact with the divine. Its influence extended beyond Uruk, serving as a model for temple architecture and religious practices throughout Mesopotamia. The complex contained multiple temples, courtyards, and administrative buildings, showcasing the advanced architectural and organizational skills of the Sumerians. The E-anna stands as a testament to the enduring power of religion and its impact on the development of early urban centers.
subtitle: House of Heaven, Temple of Inanna

keyMyths.title: Inanna's Descent to the Underworld
keyMyths.description: Inanna's journey to the underworld and subsequent return highlights her power and the cyclical nature of life and death. The E-anna served as her earthly base of power before and after her journey.

keyMyths.title: Inanna and Enki
keyMyths.description: Inanna obtains the me, divine decrees representing civilization, from Enki and brings them to Uruk, solidifying its status as a center of power. This myth emphasizes the E-anna's importance in establishing Uruk's cultural and political dominance.

sources.author: Samuel Noah Kramer
sources.work: The Sumerians: Their History, Culture, and Character
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Sumerian civilization.
sources.text: A comprehensive overview of Sumerian civilization.

sources.author: Diane Wolkstein
sources.work: Inanna: Queen of Heaven and Earth
sources.description: A retelling of Inanna's myths, including those related to E-anna.
